calendar.matters:
Request For Blocked Account
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Off Calendar

Request For Blocked Account
Off Calendar Oct-28-2015 Continued To Nov-10-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 202

Petition For Order Confirming Interest In Real Property
Off Calendar Sep-09-2015 Off Calendar

Demurrer Of Patricia Gustafson, Trustee Of The Francisca Carmela Carati Living Trust, To Petition For Order Confirming Interest In Real Property [Ccp 430.10]; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Sep-04-2015 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Confirming Interest In Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-05-2015 Continued To Sep-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Report Of Sale And Perition For Order Confirming Sale Of Real Property.
Off Calendar Jun-17-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for (trustee) Patricia Gustafson who is present. Avid Alavi for (conservtor) Thomas Lucas. Daniel Conrad for the trustee. Matthew Mraule for (objector) Carin McKay. William Simpich (objector) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that the trustee has requested a sale of real property and confirmed that Ms. Rasch has seen the objections. Mr. Conrad stated that objections have been filed by 2 parties with no standing (tenant & neighbor), that Ms. Mckay prepared a document and had Ms. Carati sign it when she did not have capacity and that this asset is the primary asset of the trustee. Mr. Mraule stated that this is more of a conservatorship issue. The court stated that that is a separate proceeding which has been heard many times. Mr. Mraule stated that the purpose of this proceeding is to cut off the rights of the tenants, that he is in agreement that the main purpose of having the court supervise the sale is to obtain the highest bid but that the process is not authorized by the Probate Code. The court stated that it is authorized under P.C. 17200 to proceed. Mr. Mraule stated that the objectors sole request is to continue the matter for time to correct the defect, that there is no authority and that they have filed an 850 petition. Mr. Simpich stated that he is permitted under the code to have standing, that Ms. Carati signed a health care directive in 2006 indicating her wish to return home and is not sure how capacity can be taken away. The court stated that this does not speak to his standing, that in the conservatorship matter the desire to return home has been looked at extensively and that none of this is relevant today or has any basis. Mr. Mraule stated that if the trustee is willing to accept the bid of the San Francisco Community Land Trust(SFCLT) he is willing to withdraw the objection. The court stated that it cannot order a trust not under court supervision to do that and that the SFCLT will be able to bid like everyone else. The court further stated that it overrules the objections, that neither objector has standing to challenge this trust, that the law allows the trust to seek direction, that it cannot tell the trustee to alter the terms of the sale and that the argument on the 2006 Health Care Directive is irrelevant, certainly to this proceeding on the trust sale. Mr. Mraule asked again for a 30 day continuance to remove the contingency and stated that the SFCLT has assurances from its financiers regarding the payment. The Court reminded Mr. Mraule that his client does not have standing and proceeded with the sale. The sale for 100% interest was confirmed at $3,280,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed 5/8/15. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. Court Reporter: Nancy E. Presant-McDonald, CSR 906, 415-421-3021. (Reported)
